channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: The Devil Is in Gradient Entanglement: Energy-Aware Gradient Coordinator for Robust Generalized Category Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2604.14176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generalized Category Discovery (GCD) leverages labeled data to categorize unlabeled samples from known or unknown classes. Most previous methods jointly optimize supervised and unsupervised objectives and achieve promising results. However, inherent o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, alignment, RAG, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Shapley Value-Guided Adaptive Ensemble Learning for Explainable Financial Fraud Detection with U.S. Regulatory Compliance Validation
item.description: arXiv:2604.14231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Financial crime costs U.S. institutions over $32 billion each year. Although AI tools for fraud detection have become more advanced, their use in real-world systems still faces a major obstacle: many of these models operate as black boxes that cannot ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, tool, model, paper, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph-Based Fraud Detection with Dual-Path Graph Filtering
item.description: arXiv:2604.14235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fraud detection on graph data can be viewed as a demanding task that requires distinguishing between different types of nodes. Because graph neural networks (GNNs) are naturally suited for processing information encoded in graph form through their mes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, embedding, model, experiment, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TOPCELL: Topology Optimization of Standard Cell via LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2604.14237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transistor topology optimization is a critical step in standard cell design, directly dictating diffusion sharing efficiency and downstream routability. However, identifying optimal topologies remains a persistent bottleneck, as conventional exhaustiv...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, library, zero-shot, LLM, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Awakening Dormant Experts:Counterfactual Routing to Mitigate MoE Hallucinations
item.description: arXiv:2604.14246v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have achieved remarkable scalability, yet they remain vulnerable to hallucinations, particularly when processing long-tail knowledge. We identify that this fragility stems from static Top-$k$ routing: routers ten...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, framework, RAG, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Explainable Graph Neural Networks for Interbank Contagion Surveillance: A Regulatory-Aligned Framework for the U.S. Banking Sector
item.description: arXiv:2604.14232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Spatial-Temporal Graph Attention Network (ST-GAT) framework was created to serve as an explainable GNN-based solution for detecting bank distress early warning signs and for conducting macro-prudential surveillance of the interbank system in the U...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, analysis, framework, ICL, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Portfolio Optimization Proxies under Label Scarcity and Regime Shifts via Bayesian and Deterministic Students under Semi-Supervised Sandwich Training
item.description: arXiv:2604.14206v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper proposes a machine learning assisted portfolio optimization framework designed for low data environments and regime uncertainty. We construct a teacher student learning pipeline in which a Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) optimizer generate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, augmented, experiment, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Verified and Targeted Explanations through Formal Methods
item.description: arXiv:2604.14209v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As deep neural networks are deployed in safety-critical domains such as autonomous driving and medical diagnosis, stakeholders need explanations that are interpretable but also trustworthy with formal guarantees. Existing XAI methods fall short: heuri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, framework, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Optimistic Policy Learning under Pessimistic Adversaries with Regret and Violation Guarantees
item.description: arXiv:2604.14243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world decision-making systems operate in environments where state transitions depend not only on the agent's actions, but also on \textbf{exogenous factors outside its control}--competing agents, environmental disturbances, or strategic adversari...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
